title: Rhaphiolepis spiralis
taxonomy: Rhaphiolepis spiralis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. This classification places it within the order Rosales, a group of flowering plants that also includes roses and apples. Rhaphiolepis spiralis is a member of the genus Rhaphiolepis, which includes several species of shrubs native to Asia and Australia. The plant is often referred to as the "spiral hawthorn" or "spiral rose bush," due to its unique spiral arrangement of leaves. Its classification as a member of the Rosaceae family reflects its close relationship to other flowering plants in this group.

In traditional medicine, the plant is used to treat fever, sore throats, and respiratory issues. It is also said to have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may contribute to its medicinal uses.
